{"content": "---
title: "The Digital Nomad's Guide to Paraguay: Cost, Community & Lifestyle"
slug: "digital-nomad-community-paraguay"
date: "[REDACTED_PHONE]"
author: "Nexa Paraguay"
category: "Digital Nomad"
excerpt: "Live in Paraguay for $1,000-1,500/month all-in. Coworking from $62-189/month, restaurant meals $5-15, gyms $30-50/month. Thriving expat communities on InterNations and Facebook. Here's your complete guide to the digital nomad lifestyle in Paraguay."
coverImage: "/images/blog/default-cover.webp"
readingMinutes: 10
draft: false
---

## The Digital Nomad's Paradise You Haven't Heard Of

Paraguay is flying under the radar for most digital nomads. While everyone flocks to Thailand, Portugal, and Mexico, a small but growing community has discovered something special here.

**The combination is unique:** zero tax on foreign income, incredibly low cost of living, fast and reliable internet, and a warm, welcoming culture. All in a country that's safe, stable, and offers a genuine path to citizenship.

## Monthly Budget Breakdown

### The All-In Cost: $1,000-1,500/month

| Category | Budget Option | Comfortable | Premium |
|----------|--------------|-------------|---------|
| **Housing** | $300-400 | $450-600 | $700-1,200 |
| **Food & Groceries** | $200-300 | $300-400 | $400-600 |
| **Eating Out** | $100-200 | $200-300 | $300-500 |
| **Utilities** | $50-80 | $80-120 | $120-150 |
| **Internet** | $25-35 | $35-50 | $50-60 |
| **Coworking** | $62-99 | $99-150 | $150-189 |
| **Transport** | $30-50 | $50-80 | $80-150 |
| **Gym** | $20-30 | $30-50 | $50-80 |
| **Healthcare** | $35-50 | $50-80 | $80-150 |
| **Miscellaneous** | $100-200 | $200-300 | $300-500 |
| **Total** | **$950-1,350** | **$1,350-2,130** | **$2,130-3,580** |

**The sweet spot for most digital nomads:** $1,200-1,600/month for a very comfortable lifestyle including a modern apartment, coworking membership, eating out regularly, and activities.

### Housing Options

| Type | Location | Monthly Rent |
|------|----------|-------------|
| Studio apartment | Asunción center | $300-450 |
| 1-bedroom | Villa Morra / Carmelitas | $400-600 |
| 2-bedroom | Las Mercedes / Centro | $500-800 |
| House with pool | Suburbs | $700-1,200 |
| Room in shared apartment | Any | $200-350 |
| Airbnb (monthly) | Asunción | $500-900 |

**Pro tip:** Sign a 6-12 month lease for the best rates. Short-term rentals (monthly Airbnb) cost 30-50% more.

### Food & Dining Costs

| Item | Cost |
|------|------|
| **Street food / mercado lunch** | $3-5 |
| **Casual restaurant meal** | $5-10 |
| **Nice restaurant (3 courses)** | $15-25 |
| **Beer (local, bottle)** | $1-2 |
| **Imported wine (bottle)** | $8-15 |
| **Coffee (cafe)** | $1.50-3 |
| **Weekly groceries (single person)** | $40-60 |
| **Weekly groceries (couple)** | $70-100 |

### Transportation

| Mode | Cost |
|------|------|
| Bus (local) | $0.25-0.50 |
| Taxi (within Asunción) | $3-8 |
| Uber/Bolt (within Asunción) | $2-6 |
| Monthly bus pass | $20-30 |
| Scooter rental (monthly) | $100-150 |
| Car rental (monthly) | $400-700 |
| Bicycle (purchase) | $150-400 |

### Fitness & Wellness

| Activity | Monthly Cost |
|----------|-------------|
| Gym membership (basic) | $20-30 |
| Gym membership (premium) | $40-80 |
| Yoga studio (unlimited classes) | $50-80 |
| Personal trainer (per session) | $15-25 |
| CrossFit box | $50-70 |

## The Digital Nomad Community

### Where to Find Fellow Nomads

| Platform/Group | Size | Activity |
|---------------|------|----------|
| **InterNations Asunción** | 2,000+ members | Monthly events, networking |
| **Paraguay Digital Nomads (Facebook)** | 1,500+ | Daily posts, meetups |
| **Expats in Paraguay (Facebook)** | 5,000+ | General expat discussion |
| **Coworking Asunción events** | Weekly | Workshops, socials |
| **Urban Station meetups** | Bi-weekly | Co-working + social |

### Who's Already Here

The expat community in Paraguay is diverse but leans toward:

| Nationality | % of Expat Community | Typical Profile |
|-------------|---------------------|-----------------|
| **German** | 25% | Retirees, entrepreneurs, families |
| **American** | 20% | Digital nomads, crypto, remote workers |
| **Dutch** | 15% | Entrepreneurs, tax-relocation |
| **British** | 10% | Remote workers, business owners |
| **Brazilian** | 10% | Cross-border business, investment |
| **Other European** | 15% | Various |
| **South African** | 5% | Remote workers, retirement |

### Languages Spoken

- **Spanish:** Essential for daily life (but you can get by with basic Spanish + English)
- **English:** Widely spoken in expat areas, coworking spaces, and upscale restaurants
- **Guarani:** Indigenous language, spoken alongside Spanish — not necessary for expats
- **German:** Surprisingly common in certain circles (colonies, business)

### Popular Expat Neighborhoods in Asunción

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Avg. Rent (1BR) | Expat Density |
|-------------|------|-----------------|---------------|
| **Villa Morra** | Upscale, restaurants, nightlife | $500-700 | High |
| **Carmelitas** | Quiet, residential, modern | $450-600 | Medium |
| **Las Mercedes** | Central, historic, walkable | $350-500 | Medium |
| **San Gerónimo** | Artsy, creative, bohemian | $300-450 | Low-Medium |
| **Herradura** | Suburban, family-oriented | $400-600 | Low |

## Lifestyle & Culture

### Climate

| Season | Months | Temperature | Humidity |
|--------|--------|-------------|----------|
| Summer | Dec - Feb | 30-40°C (86-104°F) | High |
| Autumn | Mar - May | 20-30°C (68-86°F) | Moderate |
| Winter | Jun - Aug | 10-22°C (50-72°F) | Low |
| Spring | Sep - Nov | 20-32°C (68-90°F) | Moderate |

**Best time to visit:** April-May and September-October for mild temperatures.

### Safety

| Aspect | Rating | Notes |
|--------|--------|-------|
| **Violent crime** | Low | Very rare in expat areas |
| **Petty crime** | Moderate | Pickpocketing, bag snatching in busy areas |
| **Scams targeting foreigners** | Low | Less common than in SE Asia |
| **Night safety (expat areas)** | Good | Stick to Villa Morra, Carmelitas |

**General safety tips:**
- Don't flash valuables in public
- Use Uber/Bolt at night
- Keep a copy of your passport
- Learn basic Spanish for emergencies

### Nightlife & Social Scene

| Type | Best Venues | Cost |
|------|-------------|------|
| **Beer bars** | Paseo La Galería, Bar Británico | $2-5/beer |
| **Cocktail bars** | Terraza 101, Tierra del Fuego | $8-12/cocktail |
| **Nightclubs** | Karma, Club 88, Pura Vida | $5-15 cover |
| **Live music** | Café de Acá, Sala de Ensayo | Free-$5 |
| **Cultural events** | Centro Cultural, Manzana de la Rivera | Free-$10 |

### Weekend Activities

| Activity | Location | Cost |
|----------|----------|------|
| **Sunday market (Mercado 4)** | Asunción | Free to browse |
| **Botanical Garden** | Asunción | $1 |
| **Itaipu Dam tour** | Ciudad del Este | $10-20 |
| **Iguazu Falls** (Brazil/Argentina side) | 5 hours drive | $20-30 entry |
| **Jesuit Missions** | Encarnación | $5-10 |
| **Chipa making workshop** | Various | $15-25 |
| **Yoga retreat weekend** | Caacupé | $100-200 |
| **Pantanal wildlife** | North Paraguay | $200-400 (multi-day) |

## Getting Around

### Short-Term (First Month)

**Uber/Bolt** — $2-6 per ride within Asunción. Works exactly as it does in your home country. Best option for your first month.

### Long-Term Options

| Option | Monthly Cost | Pros | Cons |
|--------|-------------|------|------|
| **Scooter** | $100-150 | Cheap, easy parking | Weather dependent |
| **Used car** | $3,000-8,000 (buy) | Freedom, air conditioning | Traffic, maintenance |
| **Car rental** | $400-700 | No commitment, new car | Expensive long-term |
| **Bicycle** | $0 (after purchase) | Healthy, zero cost | Hot, limited range |

## The #1 Thing to Know About Paraguay

**Paraguay is not a "digital nomad destination" like Bali or Lisbon.** It's a real country with real culture where people live, work, and raise families. The nomad community is small but growing.

This means:
- You won't be surrounded by influencers and laptop warriors
- You'll need to make an effort to integrate
- You'll learn Spanish faster (less English bubble)
- You'll experience authentic South American culture
- **You'll pay $0 in income tax** — which is the whole point

## Ready to Join the Community?

At Nexa Paraguay, we help digital nomads make the move:

- **Pre-arrival planning** — Housing, banking, documents
- **Arrival support** — Airport pickup, apartment viewing
- **Residency application** — Full service, 1 week
- **Community integration** — Introductions, events, groups
- **Ongoing support** — Tax guidance, legal questions

**Your $1,000/month paradise with $0 tax is waiting. Book a free consultation today.**
